content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of this project. Let me summarize what I've found:

This is a website for the **Institute of the Americas (IOA)** - a non-profit organization based at UC San Diego that promotes public policy and cooperation across the Americas. The project is built with:

- **Next.js 16.2.0** with React 19
- **Tailwind CSS 4.2** for styling
- **shadcn/ui** (Radix UI components) for UI components
- **TypeScript**
- **Lucide React** for icons

The website is a single-page app (SPA) pattern within Next.js, using `useState` for page navigation rather than Next.js routing. It has sections/pages for:

1. **Home page** - Hero, People, Programs, Featured content, News, Facilities banner, Partners, Support CTA
2. **About** - Mission, board, team members with bio modals
3. **Partners** - Partners and supporters
4. **Events** - Calendar of events
5. **Digital Library** - Publications, reports, whitepapers
6. **Facilities** - Conference spaces at UC San Diego
7. **Podcast** - "Energy Unwrapped!" podcast episodes
8. **Contact** - Contact form
9. **Donate** - Donation page

Three main programs:
- **Energy Program** (Future Energy Leaders, Energy Ambassadors, La Jolla Conference, Madrid Conference, Energy Panorama)
- **Environment Program** (Water Literacy, Marine Conservation, Sustainable Shipping, Sustainability Dispatch)
- **Economic Competitiveness** (CaliBaja Nearshoring, Leadership Academy, Critical Minerals, North American Forum)

The project name "v0_ioa" suggests this might have been generated using v0 (Vercel's AI design tool), hence the extensive use of shadcn/ui components and the SPA pattern.

Let me save this analysis as a project memory.

content.text:
Ahora tengo una comprensión completa del proyecto. Aquí está mi análisis:

---

## 📋 Análisis del Proyecto: `v0_ioa`

### ¿De qué va?

Este es el **sitio web del Institute of the Americas (IOA)** — una organización sin fines de lucro con más de 40 años de historia, afiliada a **UC San Diego** en La Jolla, California. Su misión es promover políticas públicas sound y fomentar la cooperación entre stakeholders público-privados a través del hemisferio americano, con enfoque en innovación y avance tecnológico.

### Stack Tecnológico

| Tecnología | Versión |
|---|---|
| **Next.js** | 16.2.0 |
| **React** | 19 |
| **TypeScript** | 5.7.3 |
| **Tailwind CSS** | 4.2.0 |
| **shadcn/ui** (Radix UI) | Extensa colección de componentes |
| **Lucide React** | Iconos |
| **Recharts** | Gráficas |

### Arquitectura

El nombre `v0_ioa` sugiere que fue **generado con v0** (la herramienta de diseño AI de Vercel). Usa un patrón **SPA dentro de Next.js**: toda la navegación se maneja con `useState<Page>` en `app/page.tsx` en lugar del sistema de routing de Next.js. No hay rutas dinámicas ni archivos `app/[slug]/page.tsx`.

### Páginas/Secciones del sitio

| Página | Descripción |
|---|---|
| **Home** | Hero, People, Programs, Featured, News, Facilities, Partners, Donate CTA |
| **About** | Misión, Board, Team (con bio modals) |
| **Partners** | Organizaciones asociadas y supporters |
| **Events** | Calendario de eventos (La Jolla, Madrid conferences) |
| **Library** | Biblioteca digital de publicaciones, reportes, whitepapers |
| **Facilities** | Espacios de conferencia en UC San Diego |
| **Podcast** | "Energy Unwrapped!" — episodios de podcast |
| **Contact** | Formulario de contacto |
| **Donate** | Página de donaciones |

### 3 Programas principales

1. 🔋 **Energy Program** — Política energética: Future Energy Leaders, Energy Ambassadors, La Jolla Conference, Madrid Conference, Energy Panorama
2. 💧 **Environment Program** — Sostenibilidad: Water Literacy, Marine Conservation, Sustainable Shipping, Sustainability Dispatch  
3. 🌐 **Economic Competitiveness** — Crecimiento regional: CaliBaja Nearshoring, Leadership Academy, Critical Minerals, North American Forum

### Estructura de archivos

```
app/
  page.tsx          ← Todo el routing SPA centralizado aquí
  layout.tsx
components/
  navbar.tsx        ← Mega-dropdown de Programs
  footer.tsx        ← Newsletter, social links, nav links
  sections/         ← Secciones del homepage (hero, programs, people, featured, news)
  pages/            ← Páginas individuales (about, partners, events, library, etc.)
  templates/        ← Templates reutilizables (program-template, sub-program-page)
  ui/               ← shadcn/ui components
hooks/
lib/
```

### Observaciones clave

- 🟡 **SPA pattern in Next.js**: Usa `useState` para navegación en lugar de Next.js routing — pierde beneficios como SSR por página, SEO optimizado por ruta, y lazy-loading natural
- 🟡 **Datos hardcoded**: Todos los datos (team members, events, publications) están inline en componentes — no hay API, CMS, ni data layer
- 🟡 **Sub-program pages limitadas**: Solo `water-literacy` tiene contenido detallado; las demás sub-pages probablemente necesitan expansión
- 🟢 **UI bien estructurada**: shadcn/ui completo, responsive, mega-dropdown de Programs, dark mode support
- 🟢 **Templates reutilizables**: `ProgramTemplate` y `SubProgramPage` son buenos patterns para escalar

---

¿Quieres que profundice en algún aspecto específico, o quieres que guarde este análisis en memoria para futuras conversaciones?

input.content:
---
name: ioa-project-overview
description: Overview of the v0_ioa project - Institute of the Americas website
type: project
---

# v0_ioa - Institute of the Americas Website

## What it is
Website for the **Institute of the Americas (IOA)** — a non-profit affiliated with UC San Diego, La Jolla, CA. Mission: promote sound public policy and foster cooperation across the Americas hemisphere.

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16.2.0, React 19, TypeScript 5.7.3
- Tailwind CSS 4.2.0, shadcn/ui (full Radix UI suite)
- Lucide React icons, Recharts for charts
- Likely generated with **v0** (Vercel's AI design tool)

## Architecture
- **SPA pattern inside Next.js**: All navigation via `useState<Page>` in `app/page.tsx` — no Next.js file-based routing
- All data hardcoded inline in components (no API/CMS/data layer)
- Components organized: `sections/` (homepage), `pages/` (individual pages), `templates/` (reusable), `ui/` (shadcn)

## Pages
Home, About (team/bios), Partners, Events, Library, Facilities, Podcast, Contact, Donate

## Programs (3)
1. **Energy** — Future Energy Leaders, Energy Ambassadors, La Jolla Conference, Madrid Conference, Energy Panorama
2. **Environment** — Water Literacy, Marine Conservation, Sustainable Shipping, Sustainability Dispatch
3. **Economic Competitiveness** — CaliBaja Nearshoring, Leadership Academy, Critical Minerals, North American Forum

## Key observations
- SPA pattern loses Next.js routing benefits (SSR per page, SEO, lazy loading)
- All data hardcoded — needs API/CMS integration for production
- Only `water-literacy` sub-program has detailed content
- UI is well-structured with reusable templates

# MEMORY.md

- ioa-project-overview | Overview of the v0_ioa project - Institute of the Americas website | project

content.text: Ahora modificar `handleNavigate` para que las páginas about-xxx no scrolleen al top:
Ahora modificar el switch para pasar `defaultTab` a `AboutPage`:

input.new_string:
const renderPage = () => {
    const aboutTabMap: Record<string, string> = {
      "about-board": "board",
      "about-team": "team",
      "about-advisory": "advisory",
      "about-non-resident-fellows": "non-resident-fellows",
      "about-diplomatic-fellows": "diplomatic-fellows",
    }

    if (currentPage.startsWith("about-")) {
      const tab = aboutTabMap[currentPage] || "board"
      return <AboutPage onBack={handleBack} defaultTab={tab} />
    }

    switch (currentPage) {
      case "home":
        return <HomePage onNavigate={handleNavigate} />
      case "about":
        return <AboutPage onBack={handleBack} />
      case "partners":
        return <PartnersPage onBack={handleBack} />
      case "events":
        return <EventsPage onBack={handleBack} />
      case "library":
        return <DigitalLibrary onBack={handleBack} />
      case "facilities":
        return <FacilitiesPage onBack={handleBack} onNavigate={handleNavigate} />
      case "podcast":
        return <PodcastPage onBack={handleBack} />
      case "contact":
        return <ContactPage onBack={handleBack} />
      case "donate":
        return <DonatePage onBack={handleBack} />
      default:
        return <HomePage onNavigate={handleNavigate} />
    }
  }

content.text:
✅ Listo. Push exitoso. Resumen de cambios:

### Navbar — Dropdown "About Us"
**Desktop:** Hover sobre "About Us" → dropdown con iconos y descripciones:
- 🌐 About Us → "Our mission, history, and values"
- 💼 Our Board → "Board of directors and leadership"
- 👥 Our Team → "Meet our dedicated staff"
- 🏆 Advisory Council → "Expert advisors guiding our work"
- 🎓 Non-Resident Fellows → "Distinguished contributing experts"
- 👤 Diplomatic Fellows → "Former diplomats and officials"

**Mobile:** Accordion expandible con los mismos sub-items (iconos más pequeños)

### Navegación directa a tabs
Click en cualquier sub-item → navega a la página About y:
- **"About Us"** → página normal (scroll al top)
- **"Our Board", "Our Team", etc.** → página About con el tab correcto abierto + scroll automático a la sección "Our People"

### Tipos de Page soportados
`about`, `about-board`, `about-team`, `about-advisory`, `about-non-resident-fellows`, `about-diplomatic-fellows`

content.text: The HTML is too heavy with Divi builder markup. Let me extract just the meaningful text content:

input.command:
curl -s "https://iamericas.org/partners/" | python3 -c "
import sys, re
html = sys.stdin.read()
# Remove scripts and styles
html = re.sub(r'<script[^>]*>.*?</script>', '', html, flags=re.DOTALL)
html = re.sub(r'<style[^>]*>.*?</style>', '', html, flags=re.DOTALL)
# Remove tags
text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '\n', html)
# Clean up
lines = [l.strip() for l in text.split('\n') if l.strip() and len(l.strip()) > 3]
for l in lines[:100]:
    print(l)
"

{/* Mission Statement */}

[Section 01]

Who We Are

The Institute of the Americas is an independent, inter-American institution devoted to encouraging economic and social reform in the Americas, enhancing private sector collaboration and communication, and strengthening political and economic relations between Latin America, the Caribbean, the United States and Canada.

We are committed to building bridges across the Americas — linking business leaders, policymakers, and civic leaders to share ideas and facilitate opportunities. We help understand and catalyze innovation with a focus on energy, the environment & climate change, and regional economic competitiveness on a hemispheric basis.

content.text:
✅ Push exitoso. Aquí el resumen de la nueva Partners page con 7 secciones:

| # | Sección | Descripción |
|---|---|---|
| 01 | **Who We Are** | Misión del IOA — building bridges, linking leaders, catalyzing innovation |
| 02 | **Benefits of Partnering** | 4 cards: Networking, Promotion, Research Access, Expert Insights + CTA "Partner With Us" |
| 03 | **UC San Diego Affiliation** | 5 unidades académicas con iconos (GPS, USMEX, Jacobs, Scripps, UC-TV) |
| 04 | **Our Supporters** | Dark section azul — 3 categorías: Individuals, Corporations, Foundations & Government |
| 05 | **Energy Steering Committee** | 4 miembros con cards estilo UserCircle + link "View Full Committee" |
| 06 | **Our Partner Network** | Tabs: **Institutional** (con acuerdos formales) vs **Collaborative** (sin acuerdo, joint activities). Cards con logo placeholder, badge de categoría, descripción |
| 07 | **Join Our Network** | CTA gradient azul oscuro — "Become a Partner" + "Support Our Mission" buttons |

Cambios propositivos que adicioné:
- **Benefits section** — no existía en el sitio original, muestra valor para potenciales partners
- **Steering Committee** — cards individuales con placeholder (no solo nombres)
- **Category badges** en cada partner card (Academic, Corporate, Government, etc.)
- **CTA hero buttons** — "Become a Partner" y "View Annual Report" desde el hero
- **2 CTA sections** — una después de benefits y una grande final tipo "Join Our Network"
- Todo con `onNavigate` para que los CTAs naveguen a Contact y Donate

¿Qué sigue?

Por favor, solo usa escala de grises en los colores por ahora. Este es un wireframe inicial para el nuevo website. Y la idea no es traernos tal cual lo existente del anterior sitio, sino que sea una versión mas moderna, limpia... a la que luego le aplicaremos la guia de estilos del cliente. 
Para la sección 06, no necesitamos poner tanta información, busca una manera bonita de mostrar los logos de todos. Sin tener que poner nombres debajo, ni categorias de nada, ni textos adicionales. Hazla sencilla

{/* Hero */}

Our Partners

Building bridges across the Americas — linking business leaders, policymakers, and civic leaders to share ideas and facilitate opportunities.

{/* Who We Are */}

[Section 01]

Who We Are

The Institute of the Americas is an independent, inter-American institution devoted to encouraging economic and social reform in the Americas, enhancing private sector collaboration and communication, and strengthening political and economic relations between Latin America, the Caribbean, the United States and Canada.

We are committed to building bridges across the Americas — linking business leaders, policymakers, and civic leaders to share ideas and facilitate opportunities. We help understand and catalyze innovation with a focus on energy, the environment & climate change, and regional economic competitiveness on a hemispheric basis.

content.text:
✅ Push exitoso. Cambios realizados:

### Escala de grises
- **Todos los colores** cambiados a `neutral-*` (sin blue, sin colores)
- Hero: `bg-neutral-800` + `bg-black/40` overlay
- Supporters: `bg-neutral-900`
- CTA: `bg-neutral-800`
- Buttons: `bg-neutral-900` / `bg-white text-neutral-900`
- Icon circles: `bg-neutral-100` con `text-neutral-600`
- Cards: `border-neutral-200`

### Sección 06 simplificada
- **Sin tabs**, sin badges, sin descripciones, sin categorías
- Solo 2 subtítulos minimalistas: "Institutional Partners" y "Collaborative Partners"
- **Logo grid limpio**: boxes blancos con borde `neutral-200`, `h-20`, solo placeholder text en `text-xs text-neutral-400`
- Institutional: grid `2→3→4→5` columnas (10 logos)
- Collaborative: grid `2→3→4→6` columnas (12 logos)
- Hover suave: `hover:border-neutral-300 hover:shadow-sm`

Wireframe limpio y moderno, listo para recibir la guía de estilos del cliente. ¿Qué sigue?

Usa tonos grises, hay algunas zonas que quedaron muy oscuras...(casi negras) 
Y hay 2 botones, que se ven en blando y solo al acer hover sobre ellos, se ve el texto, estan en la hero section y en la section 7.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to:
1. Use lighter gray tones - some areas are too dark (almost black)
2. Fix 2 buttons that appear white and only show text on hover - one in the hero section and one in section 7 (the CTA)

Let me look at the current partners-page.tsx to identify the issues.

2. **Section 04 Supporters**: `bg-neutral-900` = casi negro.
3. **Section 07 CTA**: `bg-neutral-800` = muy oscuro. Botón "Support Our Mission" = `variant="outline"` con `border-white/30 text-white` = invisible.
4. **Button "Partner With Us"** en Section 02: `bg-neutral-900` = casi negro.

Voy a suavizar los fondos oscuros y fixear los botones outline:

content.thinking:
The user says there are still 2 buttons with unreadable text in the hero section and section 7. Let me check the current state of the file to see exactly what's happening with those buttons.

The outline buttons likely have white text on a white/transparent background, making them invisible. The `variant="outline"` in shadcn/ui renders with a white background and border, so `text-white` would be invisible on the white button background.

Let me read the current file and fix this properly.

content.content:
Exit code: 0
STDOUT:
77:            <Button variant="outline" className="border-white text-white hover:bg-white/20 hover:text-white">
217:          <Button variant="outline" className="border-neutral-300">
284:                variant="outline"

STDERR:
75 Become a Partner 76 77 81 82 83 84

content.text: El problema es que `variant="outline"` de shadcn/ui tiene su propio color de texto default que overridea el `text-white` del className. Necesito cambiar `variant="outline"` a `variant="secondary"` o usar `variant="ghost"` que no pone borde pero tiene texto visible, o simplemente usar un estilo custom sin shadcn variant.
